title: Tender Mexican Chicken Pilaf
description: A comforting and flavorful Mexican dish featuring jasmine rice and tender chicken, perfect for a weeknight dinner. This recipe is also buddhist-temple-friendly as it does not include any animal by-products or strong-smelling ingredients.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large saucepan over medium-high heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until translucent, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional 1 minute.
Add the chicken breast pieces and cook until browned on all sides, about 5-6 minutes.
Add the cumin, paprika, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Add the jasmine rice and stir to combine with the chicken and spices.
Add the chicken broth and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the liquid is absorbed and the rice is tender.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
